normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem spec_sort (xs : List Int) : True := by trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Introduce the specification context for sorting a list of integers.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int), ∃ (ys : Vector Int xs.length), True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The result is a vector of integers whose length matches the input length.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int) (ys : Vector Int xs.length), ys.length = xs.length
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output vector has the same length as the input slice.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int) (ys : Vector Int xs.length), (∀ i j : Fin xs.length, i ≤ j → ys[i] ≤ ys[j]) → True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output is in nondecreasing order (indices respect ≤).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int) (ys : Vector Int xs.length), (ys.toList ~ xs) → True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output contains exactly the same multiset of elements as the input (permutation).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int), ∃ (ys : Vector Int xs.length), (∀ i j : Fin xs.length, i ≤ j → ys[i] ≤ ys[j]) ∧ (ys.toList ~ xs)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists an output vector of the same length that is nondecreasing and is a permutation of the input.
